title: Form 1040: U.S. Individual Income Tax Return
content: Form 1040 is the standard federal income tax form used by individuals to report their annual income and calculate their tax liability. It consolidates information from W-2s, 1099s, and other income documents. Taxpayers use it to claim the standard deduction or itemized deductions, report credits such as the Earned Income Tax Credit or Child Tax Credit, and determine whether they owe additional tax or are entitled to a refund. Nearly all U.S. citizens and resident aliens with income above the filing threshold must file a 1040 annually by April 15.
